description: Per the revised §10 (bl-2e26, 'Enforcement is CORE'), make core the enforcer and delete the standalone gating plugin shipped by bl-70f5. Core: in the claim op reject when !ready() (any unresolved claim-blocker), in the close op reject when !closeable() (any unresolved close-blocker) — sitting next to the existing already-claimed guard in change::Occupancy::stage / a close-only guard in change::Retire::stage (verb==Close; drop is never gated), using taskfile::exists as the file-existence resolver, evaluated before the seal (abort-safe) and, for close, before the delivery plugin squashes. DELETE: src/gate.rs, src/gate_tests.rs, src/bin/gate.rs, tests/gate.rs, and the 'pub mod gate;' + gate doc line in src/lib.rs. KEEP: Task::ready()/closeable(), the front-door reciprocal flags, and src/taskfile.rs (all already core). Rationale: enforcement is the MEANING of a blocker, and forge/build gates create gate children that rely on those children BLOCKING — a separate gating plugin makes them silently depend on it being installed (plugin-on-plugin dependency); core already computes the predicates, so this is a deletion plus ~two guards. Coordinate with bl-bdeb (dispatch wiring): the guards live in the claim/close op path, but can be built+tested in the change layer now (like the already-claimed guard) independent of full dispatch.
title: Move blocker enforcement into core; retire the gate plugin (§10 revision)
